comments.body: For better help sooner, post an SSCCE.
How did you add your components in the NORTH area? If you added them individually, one after another, only the last one will be available, so maybe your label wasn't really added to your panel?

** Like this:** `		constraints.setGxGyGwGhWxWyFillAnchor(4, 0, 1, 1, 1.0, 0.0, TGridBagConstraints.HORIZONTAL, TGridBagConstraints.EAST); 		gridbaglayout.setConstraints(lblBusy, constraints); 		panelStatus.add(lblBusy);`

answers.body: Most probably because the GUI was packed at a time the label was not visible, so no space was assigned to display it. For anything more definite, post an SSCCE.

comments.body: +1 for the info. If i am not mistake at point 3 (JButton and JLabel will be changed and GUI would be during), i guess you mean to write GUI would be doing instead of GUI would be during during. Regards

answers.body:
1. this is EventDispatchThread related issue, EDT quite guaranteed that all changes to the GUI would be done on one moment

  2. you invoked ActionPerformed from JButton, and untill all events ended your GUI should be freeze or is unresponsible, same for JButton and JLabel in your case

  3. better would be redirect reading for File contents to the Backgroung task e.g. SwingWorker or Runnable#Thread then JButton and JLabel will be changed and GUI would be during Background task responsible for Mouse or KeyBoard

or

  1. dirty hack split to the two separated Action delayed by javax.swing.Timer, but in this case again untill all events ended your GUI will be freeze or is unresponsible

It seems to me that you are writing lblBusy.setVisible(true); and after that lblBusy.setVisible(false); in the mouseClicked() method. Since you wanted to make it visible at the click of a button aren't you be using only lblBusy.setVisible(true);, instead of using both.

You can call lblBusy.setVisible(false); from the end of your Download Class though, once it's done doing what it does.

Regards

title: Why setVisible doesn't work?
body:
I have a swing GUI with border layout. in the NORTH I have added some component. My label component which has GIF icon is invisible lblBusy.setVisible(false); later a button make it visible like below. Why it does not show up?

btnDownload.addMouseListener(new MouseAdapter() {
        @Override
        public void mouseClicked(MouseEvent e) {
            SwingUtilities.invokeLater(new Runnable() {
                public void run() {
                    lblBusy.setVisible(true);
                    btnCancel.setEnabled(true);
                }
            });

            download = new Download(txtSource.getText(), new File(txtDestination.getText()), textAreaStatus);
            download.start();
            lblBusy.setVisible(false);
        }
    });
